elements.label: A first remote terminal receives an address and event data and uses a database to correlate the address to location-based data.
A second terminal includes a map display and receives location-based data from a location system based on the second terminal's own location.
The second terminal transmits its location-based data to the first terminal over a communication channel.
The first terminal stores the second terminal's location-based data in memory.
When the stored location-based data and the event data satisfy specified criteria, the first terminal sends the correlated location data and event data to the second terminal.
The second terminal automatically loads the received location-based data into the display unit to show a map containing the first address.
